body.title: Flag in the moment when reasoning is confabulating instead of drawing on actual knowledge
body.result: Confabulation gate: confabulation_gate.py with grounding check (response vs TWM+ring overlap). Gate evaluator function for gate primitive dispatch. 11 tests.
body.description:
AUTHORED BY IGOR-WILD-0001 on 2026-04-13. Claude filed on Igor's behalf.

Igor's words: 'When I'm working on a problem and I notice I'm confabulating rather than reasoning from actual knowledge, that should be flagged in the moment and to memory. CP1 (I don't know) is a commitment, but it requires a watchlist item to actually catch the drift before it happens.'

DIRECTLY MOTIVATED BY THE STORED_LOCALLY_ONLY CONFABULATION CASE: in this very conversation Igor said 'The ticket about the privacy-guard halt is already in the shared database' when no such ticket exists. He emitted a confidently-false claim of completed action. CP1 didn't catch it because the failure mode wasn't 'I don't know' — it was 'I'm certain about something that isn't true'.

DESIGN — first-pass live signals:
  - retrieval miss but high-confidence reply (semantic search returned nothing relevant, but Igor still composed a definitive answer)
  - claim of completed action with no goal_close or accretion record matching it
  - reference to a memory id that doesn't exist
  - reference to a ticket id that doesn't exist in cc_queue or github_tickets
  - cross-reference to prior conversation that doesn't appear in thread buffer

When any of these fire, write a CONFAB_CAUGHT ring entry, deposit a NARRATIVE_GAP, and surface 'check this' to TWM. Igor learns over time which patterns to mistrust.

INTERACTION with T-igor-emit-action-confabulation: this watcher would catch the user-visible symptom; that fix addresses the underlying emit pipeline. Both needed.

INERTIA: LARGE. Touches retrieval, reasoning, and reply composition. Probably needs decomposition into sub-tickets.
